blocking_issues: saveHostProfile writes Basic/Tunnel passwords to ESP BEFORE applyClientCertSave (which can throw on mTLS validation), so a failing save leaves credentials partially committed (cross-storage inconsistency). Pre-existing but the clientCleared reject path now reliably reaches it.
non_blocking_issues: mTLS missing-cert rejection not surfaced in the open dialog (error set on underlying screen; user sees Save 'do nothing')
clientCleared core state machine lacks tests (summary tests don't cover it)
English tunnel subtitle 'Intranet penetration' is awkward
notes: Round-3. B1 (re-toggle) CONFIRMED CLOSED via clientCleared (traced: remove→re-toggle-without-paste→hasMaterial=false→resolveClientCert null→reject, no silent old-p12 reuse; clientCleared resets only on successful paste). Error gate scoping, exactly-one-key alignment, i18n, ChatMessageContent cleanup all verified correct. Async summary no new races. New blocker is the pre-existing saveHostProfile write-ordering atomicity (now reachable via the reject path). Round-4 dispatched: orchestrator reordered applyClientCertSave before password writes; des-4 adds Save-disable-when-no-material + in-dialog reason, extracts mtlsHasMaterial pure fn + test, fixes color + copy.
